rationale: In the citing paper, Ghosh and John (2009) is not just listed in background; it is used to anchor the prior MCS governance stream that the authors claim to extend toward monitoring technologies and PPC design. The paper explicitly states that key variables are adapted from Ghosh and John (2009), and those variables are central controls in the empirical specification. This gives the cited work a meaningful conceptual and measurement role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Ghosh and John (2009) as part of the core OEM-supplier multicomponent contracting literature it builds on, then develops a new monitoring/disputes framework in that domain. It also directly reuses constructs from that paper (e.g., OEM transaction-specific investments and technological uncertainty) as model inputs, indicating methodological dependence.
